title: aspidium papuanum
intro: Aspidium papuanum, also known as the New Guinea shield fern, is a species of fern native to the island of New Guinea. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Aspidium papuanum include the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic to clean wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be applied topically or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
